page.title: Marbella Fishing Charter Cost: What to Expect for a Day Out

data.meta_description: Marbella fishing charter cost from €749 for 2h on a 12m boat. Skipper, drinks & VAT included. See what a day out costs with our fleet in Puerto Banús.
data.summary: A practical breakdown of marbella fishing charter cost, from €749 for 2 hours to €2,299 for a full day, with all-inclusive pricing. Learn what you get for your money and which boats are best for fishing off Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Fishing charters start from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
Full-day (8h) fishing trip costs €2,299 on Tier A boats.
All prices include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA.
Boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella – 15 min to fishing grounds.
Maximum 9–11 passengers depending on the boat.
Bigger yachts (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58) available from 4h minimum.
No hidden costs: no extra for ice, fishing gear, or cleaning.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations or larger vessels.

faq.q: What is the typical marbella fishing charter cost for a half-day?
faq.a: A half-day (4h) fishing charter on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) costs €1,499. This includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. You'll have time to reach prime spots like Cala del Faro or Cabopino and still be back for lunch.

faq.q: Does the price include fishing equipment?
faq.a: Yes, basic fishing rods, reels, and tackle are provided on all our charters. If you need specialised gear (e.g., for deep-sea or trolling), let us know when you book via WhatsApp and we'll arrange it at no extra cost.

faq.q: Can I keep the fish I catch?
faq.a: Absolutely. The crew will clean and bag your catch for you. There are no additional fees for keeping fish, but please check local size and bag limits – your skipper will advise on what's legal and sustainable.

faq.q: Are there cheaper options for a smaller group?
faq.a: For groups of up to 5, consider our Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m). Pricing is on request via WhatsApp, but they tend to be more affordable than the larger yachts. They also depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What's the best time of year for fishing in Marbella?
faq.a: Year-round is good, but peak season runs May to October when water temperatures hit 22–26°C. You'll target species like sea bass, bream, and bluefish. Winter brings larger dentex and amberjack. Your skipper will adapt the plan to conditions.

faq.q: Do I need a fishing license?
faq.a: No. The charter's skipper holds a commercial fishing licence that covers all passengers. You don't need to bring anything except sunscreen, a hat, and your camera.

faq.q: Can I book a private fishing charter for a special occasion?
faq.a: Yes, all our charters are private – you won't share the boat with strangers. We can add catering, a photographer, or even a barbecue on board. Just mention it when you book via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Safety comes first. If the skipper deems conditions unsafe, we'll reschedule your trip at no charge or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and advise you 24h before departure.

data.body_html:
If you're searching for "marbella fishing charter cost", you want straight numbers – not fluff. A day out on the water with us starts at €749 for 2 hours on a 12-metre Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, and goes up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. That price includes everything: a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). No surprises, no add-ons. Here's exactly what you'll pay and what you'll get.

Marbella Fishing Charter Cost by Boat Size

Our fleet in Puerto Banús covers everything from nimble speedboats to superyachts. For fishing, the sweet spot is our Tier A boats:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h, €1,499/4h, €2,299/8h
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – same pricing as above

These are sport-cruiser style boats with good deck space, a bimini for shade, and a competent skipper who knows the local grounds. For larger groups (up to 12 pax), the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) or Mangusta 80 (24m) are available from 4 hours minimum – pricing on request via WhatsApp. The Mangusta 80 even includes a jet ski free for your downtime.

What's Included in the Price

Every charter on our Boat Rental Marbella site includes:

  • Licensed skipper (fluent in English & Spanish)
  • Fuel for the full trip
  • Unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (crisps, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • 21% Spanish IVA (no tax surprises at checkout)

Fishing rods, reels, and basic tackle are included at no extra cost. If you need specialised gear (e.g., deep-sea rigs), just ask when you book.

Where You'll Fish: Best Spots Near Marbella

Departing from Puerto Banús, you're minutes from productive waters. Typical fishing grounds include:

  • Cala del Faro – rocky bottom, good for bream and sea bass
  • Cala Cortés – sheltered bay, ideal for families
  • Cabopino – reef structures holding dentex and amberjack
  • Estepona coast – deeper water for larger pelagics

Your skipper will choose based on weather, tides, and what's biting. Most trips stay within 5–10 nautical miles of the coast, so you'll never be far from shore.

How to Save on Your Fishing Charter

The best value is the 8-hour full-day charter at €2,299 – that's €287 per hour for a private boat with 9 guests, or about €255 per person for a group of 9. Compare that to a shared public fishing trip, and you get privacy, flexibility, and a better boat.

For smaller groups (up to 5 pax), consider our Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m). These are smaller but perfectly capable for inshore fishing, and pricing is typically lower – contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

Book mid-week (Monday–Thursday) and you'll often have more flexibility on timing. Peak season (June–August) weekends fill fast, so book at least a week ahead.

What Species Can You Catch?

Marbella's Mediterranean waters offer a mix of species depending on season:

  • Spring/Autumn: sea bass, red mullet, common bream
  • Summer: bluefish, bonito, mackerel, horse mackerel
  • Winter: dentex, amberjack, greater weever

Your skipper will provide the right rigs and bait. Most charters use a mix of bottom fishing and trolling. Catch-and-release is encouraged for undersized fish.

Booking Process and Cancellation Policy

To book, simply contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and duration. We'll confirm availability and send a payment link (secure card or bank transfer). A 50% deposit secures the booking; the balance is due 7 days before departure.

Cancellations are free up to 48 hours before departure. Within 48 hours, we'll try to reschedule – if that's not possible, the deposit is non-refundable. Weather cancellations are always free.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the typical marbella fishing charter cost for a half-day?

A half-day (4h) fishing charter on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) costs €1,499. This includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. You'll have time to reach prime spots like Cala del Faro or Cabopino and still be back for lunch.

Does the price include fishing equipment?

Yes, basic fishing rods, reels, and tackle are provided on all our charters. If you need specialised gear (e.g., for deep-sea or trolling), let us know when you book via WhatsApp and we'll arrange it at no extra cost.

Can I keep the fish I catch?

Absolutely. The crew will clean and bag your catch for you. There are no additional fees for keeping fish, but please check local size and bag limits – your skipper will advise on what's legal and sustainable.

Are there cheaper options for a smaller group?

For groups of up to 5, consider our Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m). Pricing is on request via WhatsApp, but they tend to be more affordable than the larger yachts. They also depart from Puerto Banús.

What's the best time of year for fishing in Marbella?

Year-round is good, but peak season runs May to October when water temperatures hit 22–26°C. You'll target species like sea bass, bream, and bluefish. Winter brings larger dentex and amberjack. Your skipper will adapt the plan to conditions.

Do I need a fishing license?

No. The charter's skipper holds a commercial fishing licence that covers all passengers. You don't need to bring anything except sunscreen, a hat, and your camera.

Can I book a private fishing charter for a special occasion?

Yes, all our charters are private – you won't share the boat with strangers. We can add catering, a photographer, or even a barbecue on board. Just mention it when you book via WhatsApp.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Safety comes first. If the skipper deems conditions unsafe, we'll reschedule your trip at no charge or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and advise you 24h before departure.

Book your Marbella fishing charter today – no hidden costs, just great value on the water.
